author: Anjney Midha
content: AI agents are moving from copilots to workflow owners. The next wave is systems that can observe, decide, and execute inside a business process with human checkpoints.
summary: Partner is discussing AI agents taking ownership of business workflows.

author: Pat Grady
content: The best AI apps are beginning to look less like chat boxes and more like full operating systems for a job function.
summary: Sequoia partner frames vertical AI systems as workflow operating systems.

author: Garry Tan
content: If you are building in robotics, AI infra, or healthcare automation and can show customer pull, YC wants to meet you.
summary: YC is explicitly encouraging founders in robotics, AI infrastructure, and healthcare automation.

author: Martin Casado
content: Inference costs are the new cloud bill. Teams that make evals, routing, caching, and observability boring will become default AI infrastructure.
summary: Partner highlights inference optimization and observability as durable AI infrastructure opportunities.

author: Diana Hu
content: The fastest-growing YC companies increasingly wedge into a painful back-office workflow, then expand into the system of record.
summary: YC partner describes vertical AI wedge patterns that can become systems of record.

author: Stephanie Zhan
content: Healthcare AI that earns trust will not be a generic assistant. It will be embedded in clinical, billing, and care coordination loops with measurable outcomes.
summary: Partner points to healthcare AI embedded in operational workflows, not generic copilots.

author: Katherine Boyle
content: Defense founders are learning to sell software with product velocity and procurement empathy. The market wants usable systems, not just demos.
summary: a16z partner emphasizes defense software teams with product speed and procurement awareness.

author: Airfront
content: We are seeing logistics teams ask for agents that can monitor exceptions, call carriers, and update customers without waiting for a dispatcher.
summary: YC-style founder signal for AI agents in logistics operations.

author: Naveen Rao
content: AI infrastructure is splitting into layers: model serving, data pipelines, evals, observability, security, and agent orchestration. Each can become a company.
summary: Sequoia partner breaks down AI infrastructure into investable layers.

author: Y Combinator
content: Request for Startups: AI tools for nurses, care coordinators, and revenue cycle teams that reduce administrative burden in healthcare.
summary: YC RFS explicitly asks for healthcare AI reducing administrative burden.

author: Marc Andreessen
content: The boring vertical workflow that saves ten hours a week is often a better AI company than the dazzling demo.
summary: Partner reinforces pragmatic vertical AI workflows over broad demo products.

author: MedBill AI
content: We launched a denial management agent that reads payer policy, drafts appeals, and learns from revenue cycle team feedback.
summary: Founder signal for healthcare back-office automation with agentic workflow learning.

author: Shaun Maguire
content: Defense tech is becoming a software market with urgent customers, modern buyers, and a new founder profile.
summary: Sequoia partner describes defense tech as a faster-moving software market.

author: BuildOps AI
content: Field service companies want AI that schedules techs, orders parts, and follows up with customers from the same workflow.
summary: Founder describes vertical AI demand in field service operations.

author: Harj Taggar
content: Developer tools with obvious ROI are still winning. AI helps most when it shortens debugging, deployment, and compliance work that teams already pay for.
summary: YC partner highlights ROI-driven AI developer tools.

author: EvalGrid
content: EvalGrid helps AI teams compare agent runs across cost, latency, tool use, and customer outcome metrics.
summary: Founder launch in eval tooling for AI agents and infrastructure teams.
